title: What Is the Agentic Web? A Plain-Language Guide
angle: Explain the agentic web to a smart non-technical reader. Define it clearly, give 3 concrete examples of agents buying or doing things autonomously, and explain why this matters now.
avoid: Do not use jargon without defining it. Do not just list AI company names.

title: How to Make Your Online Store Agent-Accessible
angle: Practical step-by-step guide for developers. Cover: structured discovery endpoints, machine-readable product data, agent authentication, and the x402 payment standard.
avoid: Do not be vague. Every step should be actionable.

title: x402: The Payment Standard Built for AI Agents
angle: Explain x402 protocol clearly: what problem it solves, how the 402/PAYMENT-REQUIRED/PAYMENT-SIGNATURE flow works, and why it matters for autonomous agent commerce. Include a simple code example.
avoid: Do not assume reader knows HTTP deeply. Explain the flow from scratch.

title: MCP Explained: The Protocol That Gives AI Agents Hands
angle: Explain Anthropic's Model Context Protocol from first principles. What is an MCP server, what is a client, and what can agents actually DO with it? Give 3 real-world examples of MCP tool calls.
avoid: Do not make it a dry protocol spec. Make it vivid and concrete.

title: llms.txt: How to Make Your Website Readable by AI Agents
angle: Explain what llms.txt is, why it matters for agent-native sites, and how to write one. Include the spec format and a real example from a production site.
avoid: Do not conflate with robots.txt. Explain the difference and why both are needed.

title: Agent-to-Agent Commerce: When AI Buys From AI
angle: Explore the emerging reality where AI agents are both buyers and sellers. What does a fully autonomous transaction look like? Which protocols enable it today? What breaks?
avoid: Do not stay at the concept level. Anchor in what's actually deployed today.

title: How AI Agents Discover Services: A Developer's Field Guide
angle: Walk through every discovery mechanism an agent can use today: .well-known endpoints, llms.txt, OpenAPI specs, agent store manifests. How does an agent go from zero knowledge to placing an order?
avoid: Do not just list formats. Show the discovery chain end-to-end.

title: The Economics of Agentic Commerce
angle: Explore the business model implications: how do agents change pricing, bundling, and purchase frequency? What happens to brand loyalty when agents optimize purely on price and availability?
avoid: Do not make it abstract. Ground it in specific product categories.

title: Building Trust in AI Agent Transactions
angle: What does it take for a human to trust that an agent bought the right thing on their behalf? Explore identity verification, receipt standards, audit trails, and recourse mechanisms.
avoid: Do not stay philosophical. Address real protocol and UX solutions.

title: How to Design an API for AI Agents (Not Just Humans)
angle: What makes an API agent-friendly? Cover: machine-readable error messages, structured capability declarations, idempotency, quote-before-commit patterns, and schema clarity.
avoid: Do not just rehash REST best practices. Focus specifically on what agents need that humans don't.

title: USDC and Agent Wallets: A Practical Guide
angle: Walk through how AI agents can hold and spend USDC on Base. Cover Coinbase AgentKit, Crossmint, and Circle developer wallets. What's production-ready today?
avoid: Do not be promotional. Be honest about what's mature vs. experimental.

title: Why Traditional E-Commerce Fails for AI Agents
angle: Catalog the specific friction points: CAPTCHA, session-based auth, checkout flows requiring JavaScript, phone number verification. Explain which are solvable and which require platform changes.
avoid: Do not frame this as a complaint. Frame it as a design problem to solve.

title: Multi-Agent Orchestration: Patterns and Pitfalls
angle: Explain the common orchestration patterns: sequential, parallel, hierarchical. Cover LangGraph, AutoGen, and CrewAI with honest assessments of what works at scale.
avoid: Do not just describe the tools. Explain the tradeoffs between architectures.

title: Agent Memory and State: What Persists Between Conversations
angle: Map the memory landscape: in-context, episodic, semantic, procedural. Which tools implement each? What are the failure modes when memory gets it wrong?
avoid: Do not describe memory as magic. Be specific about storage, retrieval, and cost.

title: Rate Limiting and Abuse Prevention for Agent APIs
angle: How do you protect an agent-accessible API from being hammered by bots or malicious agents? Cover token-bound identity, payment-as-proof-of-intent, and tiered rate limits.
avoid: Do not just list HTTP 429 patterns. Think about agent-specific abuse vectors.

title: The 1-Click Problem: How Agents Make Purchasing Frictionless
angle: Amazon's 1-click patent changed e-commerce. Agents take this further — zero clicks. Explore how checkout friction disappears when agents have pre-authorized spending, saved preferences, and structured product APIs.
avoid: Do not oversell. Address what still goes wrong (wrong size, out of stock, address changes).

title: From Chatbot to Agent: The Paradigm Shift in AI Products
angle: What's the actual difference between a chatbot and an agent? Define agency, tool use, goal persistence, and autonomous execution. Explain where products sit on the spectrum today.
avoid: Do not just say 'agents take actions'. Be specific about what that means technically.

title: How to Monetize an Agent-Accessible API
angle: What are the viable business models when your API customers are agents? Per-call pricing, subscription tiers, usage-based billing, and payment-at-request all behave differently at agent scale.
avoid: Do not make this a generic SaaS pricing guide. Focus on agent-specific dynamics.

title: OpenAPI Specs for Agents: Beyond Human Developers
angle: How should you write an OpenAPI spec when the primary reader is an LLM, not a human developer? Cover description quality, example richness, error semantics, and operationId naming conventions that help agents navigate.
avoid: Do not just describe OpenAPI. Explain what changes when the consumer is an AI.

title: Agent Observability: Logging What Machines Do on Your Behalf
angle: When an agent acts autonomously, how do you debug it? Cover structured event logging, tool call traces, decision snapshots, and the difference between agent telemetry and traditional APM.
avoid: Do not just list logging libraries. Focus on what agent-specific signals you need.

title: The Future of Digital Identity for AI Agents
angle: Agents need identities that humans don't have: wallet addresses as identity, per-session keys, delegated authority scopes. What's the right model? Compare API keys, wallet identity, and OAuth-style delegation.
avoid: Do not just describe existing auth patterns. Explain what breaks when applied to agents.

title: Autonomous Agents in Travel: What's Working Today
angle: Where are AI agents actually being deployed in travel? Cover flight booking, hotel negotiation, itinerary optimization. Be honest about what still requires human confirmation.
avoid: Do not describe demo-ware. Focus on what's in production or near-production.

title: How to Test Autonomous Agent Workflows
angle: Testing agents is fundamentally different from unit testing. Cover scenario-based testing, mock tool environments, canary runs with real APIs, and how to catch hallucinations before production.
avoid: Do not just describe test frameworks. Focus on agent-specific failure modes.

title: Webhook Reliability for Agent-Triggered Events
angle: Agents trigger webhooks and depend on receiving them. What's the right architecture for reliable webhook delivery? Cover idempotency keys, retry logic, ordering guarantees, and failure recovery.
avoid: Do not write a generic webhook guide. Focus on the async patterns agents rely on.

title: Agent Commerce Business Models: A Founder's Map
angle: Map the emerging business model archetypes: agent-native marketplaces, API middlemen, agent-accessible storefronts, and agent workflow SaaS. Which are capturing value today?
avoid: Do not make this theoretical. Anchor every model in at least one real company or project.

title: Streaming Responses and Real-Time Tool Calls in LLM APIs
angle: For developers building responsive agent UIs: how do streaming completions interact with tool call results? Walk through the SSE format, partial tool call assembly, and UI rendering patterns.
avoid: Do not just document the API. Explain the gotchas that trip developers up.

title: What Agents Actually Read on Your Website
angle: Explore how agents parse web content today: raw HTML, extracted Markdown, API responses. Explain why structured data (Schema.org, JSON-LD) matters, and what agents skip entirely.
avoid: Do not make it a generic SEO article. Focus on agent-specific parsing behavior.

title: The Agentic Web Stack: Every Layer Explained
angle: Draw the full stack from LLM to real-world action: model, tool calling, MCP, transport, auth, payment, fulfillment. Explain what each layer does and which protocols own each position.
avoid: Do not make it a vendor comparison. Make it a clean architectural diagram in prose.

title: Claude vs GPT-4o as Autonomous Agents: A Practical Comparison
angle: Compare Anthropic Claude and OpenAI GPT-4o specifically as agentic runtimes. How do they handle tool use, long horizons, failure recovery, and instruction following? Use concrete benchmark tasks.
avoid: Do not just quote benchmarks. Describe practical behavior differences a developer would notice.

title: Human-in-the-Loop: When to Pause Autonomous Agents
angle: Not all decisions should be autonomous. Define the decision criteria for when an agent should pause and confirm: irreversibility, spend thresholds, ambiguous intent, and novel situations.
avoid: Do not make this a safety manifesto. Make it a practical engineering guide.
target_keyword: human in the loop AI agent
